Cardinality.description: How many bindings may fill a method input slot.
Cardinality.title: Cardinality

value.description: The code value within the system. May be null when not yet assigned.

Coding.description: §3.3 — a (system, value) coding with optional human label. Used in `codings[]` for supplementary codes beyond the primary `code`.
Coding.title: Coding

Configuration.description: A configuration option on a method or on a method output. Values are supplied by transformations via `methodConfigurations[].value`.

applicable_scopes.description: Optional list of scopes where this configuration applies (e.g. ['universal'], ['per_test']).

conforms_to.description: Optional FK into a controlled vocabulary (e.g. "alpha", "multiplicity_adjustment") for reusable configuration semantics.

defaultValue.description: Default value if not supplied by the transformation. Type matches dataType.

name.description: Configuration identifier within the parent's configurations[]. Referenced from transformation `methodConfigurations[].configurationName`.

Configuration.title: Configuration

ConfigurationDataType.description: Data type of a method or output configuration value.
ConfigurationDataType.title: ConfigurationDataType

Distribution.description: §3.5.3 — the probabilistic basis of the test statistic.
Distribution.title: Distribution

FHIRValueType.description: FHIR datatype carried by a method output. Used by derivation outputs (§3.6). Analysis outputs use output_type FK into output_class_templates instead.
FHIRValueType.title: FHIRValueType

Formula.description: §3.4 — the formula contract. The generic_expression's <token> identifiers MUST appear in the parent method's methodInput[].name.

generic_expression.description: Pattern showing input slots in angle brackets (e.g. '<response> ~ <covariate>* + <fixed_effect>+').

Formula.title: Formula

FormulaNotation.description: The grammar used by the method's formula. See lib/vocabulary/formula_grammar.json.
FormulaNotation.title: FormulaNotation

Method.description: Top-level structure of an M_*.json file. Per §3.2, methods carry identity (§3.3), a formula contract (§3.4), input/output slots, and configurations. They never reference clinical or analysis concepts.

assumptions.description: Free-text method assumptions (e.g. "Pre value is non-zero"). Reserved for future machine-readability (§7 open question).

class.description: Free-text method family (e.g. "General Linear Models", "Summary", "Data Handling").

code.description: §3.3 — primary code (typically NCI). value may be null when not yet assigned.

conceptId.description: §3.3 — the canonical identifier (e.g. M.ANCOVA). Replaces the legacy `name` field on existing M_*.json files.

configurations.description: Method-level configuration options. Per-output configurations live on MethodOutput.

formula.description: §3.4 — formula declaration. Token names in generic_expression MUST resolve to methodInput[].name values.

label.description: §3.2 — human-readable label. Used by SmartPhrase rendering.

methodInput.description: §3.4 — input slots. Slot names are formula tokens AND transformation-binding FK targets.

methodOutput.description: §3.5/§3.6 — output slots. Slot names are transformation-binding FK targets.

schema_version.description: Version of this LinkML schema the file conforms to.

shortLabel.description: §3.2 — compact label. Used by SmartPhrase rendering when a tight form is needed.

type.description: Whether this method is an analysis or a derivation.

Method.title: Method

MethodInput.description: §3.4 — an input slot on the method. `name` is both a formula token and a transformation-binding FK target.

name.description: Slot identifier within methodInput[]. Appears in formula.generic_expression as <name> and in transformation bindings as methodInput.

MethodInput.title: MethodInput

MethodInputDataType.description: Expected data type of a method input slot.
MethodInputDataType.title: MethodInputDataType

MethodOutput.description: §3.5/§3.6 — an output slot on the method. For analysis methods, `output_type` references an entry in output_class_templates.json (which decomposes into output_class + output_shape + distribution per §3.5). For derivation methods, `value_type` and `unit_policy` give the direct value type (§3.6).

configurations.description: Per-output configurations (e.g. multiplicity_adjustment on p-value outputs).

indexed_by.description: For multi-row outputs (e.g. one row per factor level), the methodInput names whose levels index the output rows. May include interaction tokens (e.g. "fixed_effect:fixed_effect").

name.description: Slot identifier within methodOutput[]. Referenced from transformation outputDataStructure.measures[].methodOutput.

output_type.description: FK into lib/vocabulary/output_class_templates.json. Identifies a (class, shape, distribution) tuple per §3.5. Typically used by analysis methods.

unit_policy.description: §3.6.1 — per-output unit-handling policy for derivation outputs.

value_type.description: §3.6 — FHIR value type for derivation outputs (e.g. fhir:Quantity).

MethodOutput.title: MethodOutput

MethodType.description: Whether this method is an analysis or a derivation.
MethodType.title: MethodType

OutputClass.description: §3.5.1 — the inferential ROLE of an analysis output (the "what is it for"). Vocabulary anchor for output_class_templates.json values.
OutputClass.title: OutputClass

OutputShape.description: §3.5.2 — the inferential PATTERN (the structure of the result).
OutputShape.title: OutputShape

UnitPolicy.description: How a derivation method handles units on its output. Per §3.6.1, this is a per-output property (lives on MethodOutput, not Method).
UnitPolicy.title: UnitPolicy

description: Top-level structure of an M_*.json file. Per §3.2, methods carry identity (§3.3), a formula contract (§3.4), input/output slots, and configurations. They never reference clinical or analysis concepts.

assumptions.description: Free-text method assumptions (e.g. "Pre value is non-zero"). Reserved for future machine-readability (§7 open question).

class.description: Free-text method family (e.g. "General Linear Models", "Summary", "Data Handling").

code.description: §3.3 — primary code (typically NCI). value may be null when not yet assigned.

conceptId.description: §3.3 — the canonical identifier (e.g. M.ANCOVA). Replaces the legacy `name` field on existing M_*.json files.

configurations.description: Method-level configuration options. Per-output configurations live on MethodOutput.

formula.description: §3.4 — formula declaration. Token names in generic_expression MUST resolve to methodInput[].name values.

label.description: §3.2 — human-readable label. Used by SmartPhrase rendering.

methodInput.description: §3.4 — input slots. Slot names are formula tokens AND transformation-binding FK targets.

methodOutput.description: §3.5/§3.6 — output slots. Slot names are transformation-binding FK targets.

schema_version.description: Version of this LinkML schema the file conforms to.

shortLabel.description: §3.2 — compact label. Used by SmartPhrase rendering when a tight form is needed.

type.description: Whether this method is an analysis or a derivation.

title: acdc_method
